𒐊𒄰 𒀭𒋫 𒆷𒁀𒄖
𒇽 𒌑𒊏 𒆠𒋫 𒆪𒆪 ...
entries.structure.message.altar:
The stone altar is worn smooth.

Under layers of ash and blood, someone later carved cable grooves into it.
entries.structure.message.altar_2:
The altar face dips shallowly, dull with a film of dried oil.

Small notches ring the edge, cut not by fingers but by tools or claws.
entries.structure.message.altar_3:
Only the four corners of the altar remain intact.

The center was gouged away, then lined later with clips and a row of narrow ports.
entries.structure.message.altar_4:
The pale stone stays cold, but the channels alone return a trace of warmth.

Marks from an older devotion are crossed by symbols from a later system.
entries.structure.message.temple_core:
The exposed core of a ruined nave stands here.

Fractured floor channels still return a trace of old prayer-heat, suggesting something remains sealed under the rubble.
entries.structure.message.temple_core_2:
Only the inside of the fallen colonnade still holds as a single chamber.

The floor seams look less like ornament and more like the frame of something once fixed in place.
entries.structure.message.temple_core_3:
The nave has collapsed, yet the central core alone remains unnaturally settled.

A black resin-like filler runs through the joins in the stone, clearly poured in later.
entries.structure.message.temple_core_4:
The core-stone exposed under the rubble resembles a dissected organ more than a sanctuary.

Prayer paths and cable traces mingle in the floor, impossible to separate by age.
entries.structure.message.substory_core:
The sutures in the walls look half like stone and half like flesh.

Wind-vents and brood-bed hollows make this place feel nothing like a human laboratory.
entries.structure.message.terminal:
The terminal chassis still carries power.

The screen alternates between wiring diagrams and subject numbers.
entries.structure.message.vending_machine:
An obsolete intake slot and selection panel remain on the machine face.

The display is dark, but the recognition core inside has not fully fallen silent.
entries.structure.message.bone_rack:
Standard-cut bones hang from the rack.

Tags list spine, rib, and phalanx in the same format as machine parts.
entries.structure.message.cable_pylon:
The pylon's sheath has split open and bundled lines pulse inside.

Each pulse is drawn downward in sync toward something deeper.

entries.inventory.no_selected_help: Select an item from the left list.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_knowledge:
[SCIENTIA-03/07 | restore 31%]
Recovered from the Apostolic Signal Lattice. Restored lines note paired handling of Subjects III and IV and symbolic-response trials through the Catechism Gate.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_life:
[VITA-05/07 | restore 27%]
Recovered from the Helix Perfusion Cradle. Cross-feed between the sibling subjects stabilized, yet neither developed a dedicated offensive morphology under vascular rehearsal.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_dimension:
[DIMENSIO-02/11 | restore 18%]
Fragmentary log from the Shear Ring Annex. Pre-awakening specimen recovered suspended from a cavern ceiling organ and transferred to the Orthogonal Coupling Frame.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_interface:
[INTERFACE-04/09 | restore 22%]
Bridge test from the Litany Relay Spine. Subject V did not respond to spoken prompts, but the cranial halo accepted twelve seconds of one-way handshake through Port AURELIA.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_extractio:
[EXTRACT-06/10 | restore 19%]
Readout from the Pneuma Bore Array. Filament taps recovered non-linguistic signal clusters from peri-cardial tissue; operators tagged them as key-bearing residues.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_archive:
[ARCHIVE-02/05 | restore 44%]
Vault index copied from the Sepulcher Stack. Entries list Oriens and Occasus as devotional epochs, then reclassify all later apostolic remains under vivisection and systems intake.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_cathedral:
[CATHEDRAL-08/12 | restore 16%]
Maintenance strip from the Cathedral Engine. Frame-bells were tuned to match Subject II impact harmonics before release into the lower nave corridor.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_halo:
[HALO-01/08 | restore 39%]
Cranial assembly note. A six-spoke halo crown was seated over the cervical ring and pinned to the Catechism Gate bus for passive key listening.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_lung:
[LUNG-03/06 | restore 28%]
Flow report from the Choral Lung Tower. Pressure organs grown for Subject IV continued singing after tissue separation and were rerouted into coolant hymn lines.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_ossuary:
[OSSUARY-07/09 | restore 24%]
Bone inventory from the Ossuary Rail. Later apostolic remains were sawn into standard lengths to fit carriage teeth and vertebral indexing clamps.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_choir:
[CHOIR-05/11 | restore 21%]
Acoustic capture log. The Resonance Choir Array produced stable echo maps only when attendants recited Oriens liturgies through a blood-warm diaphragm cone.
entries.item.description.quartz_memory_witness:
[WITNESS-02/04 | restore 51%]
Human testimony filed into the Witness Vault. Surviving staff described Subject V as sleeping, but all monitor petals had already opened toward the Orthogonal Coupling Frame.
entries.item.description.ember_scroll: Launches a cinder line that burns the target. The scroll is not consumed.
entries.item.description.bind_scroll: Launches a binding bolt that slows the target. The scroll is not consumed.
entries.item.description.repulse_scroll: Emits a repulsive wave around the caster, pushing back and slowing nearby enemies. The scroll is not consumed.
entries.item.description.pulse_bomb: A close-range dispersal charge. Damages and staggers enemies crowding the user.
entries.item.description.gladius_nadir: An old blade that grows heavier near deep machinery.
entries.item.description.ferrum_occasus: A ritual sword that remembers the shock of broken ranks.
entries.item.description.virga_oriens: An old staff marked by cinder-lit mercy.
entries.item.description.virga_meridies: A living rod that answers with restraint rather than force.
entries.item.description.virga_zenith: A staff that slips the body a short distance around sealed stone.

entries.hints.title: Hints
entries.hints.1: - Use V to open this menu.
entries.hints.3: - Attack chain resets if a non-attack turn passes.
entries.hints.5: - Stone Axe recipe uses stone + wood + string.
entries.hints.6: - Use Stone Axe from inventory to equip it.
entries.dialogue.traveler.flee: Stay back! Just leave me alone!
entries.dialogue.traveler.attacked: W-what are you doing?! Stay away!
entries.ancient.trace_gain: The echo of an old prayer settles within you.
entries.ancient.trace_awakened: A fading voice in the ruin answers, just barely.
entries.ancient.insufficient: The ruin answers, but it does not open yet.
entries.ancient.spent: Only ash and silence remain here now.
entries.ancient.zenith_fail: No seam through the stone answers your hand.
entries.ancient.zenith_step: Your body slips through stone and emerges at ({x}, {y}).
entries.facility.cleared: Something deep inside the aberrant facility falls silent.
